intro: Panel-data causal econometrics: adoption paths, cell-level potential outcomes, fixed effects, residualization and weighted-regression infrastructure, and estimand-characterization results for DiD, event-study, and TWFE designs.

namespace_intros.Analysis: Population residualization for Frisch-Waugh-Lovell estimand characterization: linear nuisance classes in L²(μ), residualization witnesses, and the residualized regression coefficient characterized from the normal equations.
namespace_intros.Assignment: Treatment-assignment processes for panels: staggered adoption, absorbing states, and the assignment σ-algebras conditioning panel estimands.
namespace_intros.Baseline: Baseline outcome models for panels (two-way fixed effects, group-time baselines) against which treatment effects are measured.
namespace_intros.DGP: Panel data-generating processes: static and additive-lag outcome models with explicit potential-outcome semantics.
namespace_intros.EstimandCharacterization: What popular panel regressions actually estimate: formal characterizations of TWFE and event-study estimands from the recent difference-in-differences literature.
namespace_intros.EstimandCharacterization.ImputationEvent: Borusyak-Jaravel-Spiess: the imputation estimator and the efficiency/characterization results for staggered adoption.
namespace_intros.EstimandCharacterization.HeterogeneousTW: de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfœuille: negative-weight diagnostics for TWFE under heterogeneous effects.
namespace_intros.EstimandCharacterization.StaggeredTWFEDe: The Goodman-Bacon decomposition: TWFE-DiD as a weighted average of 2x2 comparisons, with the weight formulas.
Bridges expressing the Goodman-Bacon decomposition in the project's panel regression substrate.

Support layer for the Goodman-Bacon decomposition: saturated cohort-period classes, cell-mass identities, orthogonality lemmas, residualization witnesses, and per-cell numerator and denominator formulas.
namespace_intros.EstimandCharacterization.OLSWeightDecomp: Sloczynski's characterization: the OLS coefficient as a convex combination of group-specific effects with reversed weights.
The finite-cell headline: the saturated-OLS residualized coefficient equals the overlap-weighted ATE built from real potential outcomes.
Per-cell measure-theoretic machinery supporting Sloczynski's weighting result in the project's regression substrate.
namespace_intros.EstimandCharacterization.EventStudyConta: Sun-Abraham: contamination of event-study coefficients under heterogeneous cohort effects and the interaction-weighted fix.
namespace_intros.EstimandCharacterization.FlexibleDIDMund: Wooldridge's extended TWFE: the equivalence of pooled OLS with saturated interactions and imputation-style estimands.
namespace_intros.FixedEffect: Fixed-effect projection algebra: the within transformation and its annihilator properties.
namespace_intros.PO: Potential-outcome semantics for panels: cell-level systems, exposure mappings, treatment-history responses, and panel consistency.
namespace_intros.PathLimit: Probability limits of panel regression objects along treatment paths: population Gram matrices, score contrasts, and convergence interfaces.
namespace_intros.Probability: Probability substrate for panel asymptotics: product spaces, iid Bernoulli assignment, sampling, and population moments.
namespace_intros.Probability.IIDBernoulli: iid Bernoulli assignment processes and their moment computations.
namespace_intros.Probability.Pi: Product (Π-type) probability spaces underlying cross-sectional sampling of panel units.
namespace_intros.Probability.PopulationMoments: Population moments of panel observables as integrals over the unit-level distribution.
namespace_intros.Probability.Sample: Finite-sample objects (sample means, sample Gram matrices) and their population counterparts.
namespace_intros.Regression: Panel regression infrastructure: design matrices, OLS solutions, residual algebra, homogeneous-collapse lemmas, and closed-form estimand formulas for canonical specifications.
namespace_intros.Regression.Formulas: Closed-form estimand formulas for canonical panel specifications (static TWFE, event studies, duration designs, switch designs).
namespace_intros.Target: Named panel target functionals (history contrasts, carry-over effects, duration profiles) and their evaluation under structural shape assumptions.
namespace_intros.Weighted: Weighted panel estimators: weighting schemes over cohorts/periods and the estimands they aggregate to.
